description: A guy walked up and tried to convince me that this was an ugly building.  There are prettier ones in London.  But this massive brick blob appealed to me.

name: In back of Geffrye's museum, blue wall, elevated railway arches. (c1)
description:
I was getting off on the garages and offices and business tucked under the arches of the rail line.

In another part of London was an incredible vibrant market street, all under arches.

name: Ives Pool, Ives Park, Sebastopol, CA
description: Ives pools is where the Sebastopol Sea Serpents swim.  It was the first place I added to Platial (not because it is that special, but because it is the place closest to my house, which is not my house, which is worth adding to a map.)

name: Modern apartment near the Geffrye's museum (c4)
description: The second or third thing I notice when I travel to the 'old' world is the intertangling of old and new.  The sherbet booth set up between the stones of ancient monuments.  The Modern architecture next to the Victorian (and to be honest, the faux/recreated Victorian) architecture.  The interplay of flavors, the pastiche of culture and time.  Time Pudding.

St Anne's Church, London, UK (b2)

Countryside near Hursley, UK (a10)
description: I went on a bit of a walkabout outside of Hursley, and discovered why the English talk of 'walking holidays' and why they value walking through the countryside.  Short answer: paths and trails and beautiful country.

name: Hursley and the King's Head (a1)
description:
There is the little village of Hursley, captured in a panorama.  There is me hanging out while it goes click click.  There are artifacts of passing cars, and the 360 degree artifacts.
And a driver-by "That's a dangerous place to be with me driving" - a commentary on his driving, not on my location.

name: A modern (?) faux castle from the beach in Calshot
Beach houses in Calshot, UK (p5)
description: Beach houses in Calshot, UK.  "Very English Tradition, these Beach Houses"

name: Lychgate, Hursley, UK
description:
A Lychgate is covered gate into a traditional British cemetery.

Check out the wikipedia article: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lychgate

It is formally (I hear :-) considered a part of the church.  The coffin, or the body if uncoffined, is layed down in the lychgate and some part of the ceremony is conducted there.  It is also the place for the pall bearers to be protected from the rain.

name: Countryside outside Hursley, UK (a15)
description: The golden countryside outside of Hursley

name: All Saints Church, Hursely, UK (a8)
description:
All Saints Church, Hursley, is another of those little Parish churchs which, to pardon the cliche, are dotted about the english countryside like freckles on someone who has freckles.

I spend a disproportionate amount of time poking into the side bits of the  churchyard.

name: Two o'clock and the trash fence, burning man 2007
description: Out out the Two O'Clock radial, out to the trash fence.  A 360 of near emptiness.  But in the emptiness there is structure, things appear - the horns spray painted on my back, the two copies of the swan - my bike was in the first and last columns of the 360 and it didn't stitch right.  And the vignetting, always the vignetting when I am at burning man.

name: My driveway, getting ready for Burning Man 2007
Sonoma County California Coastline  (coast1)
description: The coastline rocks...

name: Burning Man 2007 Treehouse
description:
The treehouse at Burning Man.  It probably had some official name (like the oil derrick was 'really' called 'Crude Awakening').  It was cool.

The guy sitting on the left branch is one of the builders.

name: burning man 2007 - Sunrise
description:
Panorama taken from our swan from in front of our camp site in Fandango.  The Fandango bar features prominently.

One could wonder what I was doing up at sunrise, but that would be an indelicate question.

name: Gerstle Cove, Sonoma County (coast7)
description: I had to wade a bit to get to the rocks from which I took this panorama.  Gerstle cove is a no take reserve, and popular diving spot.  Molly and her 'friend' dove while I took pictures.

name: IBM Hursley (p1)
description:
This was once Oliver Cromwell's estate, and so once the 'country' seat of government in England.  The house was different then.  Now it is an IBM facility.

This was the first gigapan I took after first playing with the GigaPan at Foo Camp.

name: Supplicant figure at Burning Man 2007
description:
One of the supplicant figures-I thought this was related to the oil derrick, but it was nowhere near to it.

Exact location and time to be added once I get my GPS track logs all loaded.

name: O'Reilly
description:
Sunday morning, early, sunrise at the O'Reilly campus in Sebastopol.

The truck at the left is Chicken John's truck which runs on Coffee grounds.
